hypotheses.label: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural splice/recurse mutators place MORE DISTINCT coherent structural tokens/constructs per seed than cmplog's I2S byte-substitution can, even though cmplog can place individual literals

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: Use joint_necessity's GENERIC 2-arm analyze_pair (already in the tool; --winner-fuzzer grimoire --loser-fuzzer cmplog), NOT the default vpc/cmplog/vp 3-arm mode. The decisive pair for this shape is grimoire>cmplog (every card's covers_pairs == 'grimoire>cmplog (grimoire_structural)'), so the blocking arm is CMPLOG, not naive. This is the round-1 fix: round-0 contrasted grimoire-vs-naive, but naive actually RESOLVED most harfbuzz branches (naive seeds are in resolving_seeds, not blocking_seeds), so the naive contrast was both wrong-arm and self-weakening. cmplog is present in blocking_seeds for ALL 6 measurable harfbuzz branches. analyze_pair emits canonical winner_tags/loser_tags/tag_lift/size_lift plus armtag aliases grimoire_tags/cmplog_tags. tag_lift is a DIFFERENCE (grimoire_tags - cmplog_tags) of mean distinct-token counts; size_lift is the median-size RATIO grimoire/cmplog.

params.tokens_note: harfbuzz tokens are the 4-byte sfnt table tags + sfnt/ttc magic ('true','ttcf','OTTO') the GeneralizationStage gaps-and-preserves as coherent runs (see harfbuzz_5440_h2 preserving 'true'/'loca'/'ttcf'; harfbuzz_6181 multi-table recombination). The per-target token vocab is a measurement PARAM, not a new descriptor.

hypotheses.prediction: grimoire_tags - cmplog_tags >= 1.0: a grimoire seed carries on average at least one MORE distinct sfnt-structural token than a cmplog blocking seed. The discriminating alternative this rules out is BYTE-RICHNESS-NOT-STRUCTURE: cmplog runs I2S, so if the advantage were merely placing magic/tag LITERALS (a byte-level capability cmplog ALSO has via input-to-state), cmplog_tags would match grimoire_tags and tag_lift -> 0. A positive tag_lift against the I2S arm (not the naive arm) isolates grammar-aware multi-token RECOMBINATION from literal substitution. size_lift >= 1.0 corroborates that grimoire's coherent structure is at least as large as cmplog's stalled inputs.
hypotheses.rule_explanation: tag_lift (= grimoire_tags - cmplog_tags) >= 1.0 means grimoire seeds carry on average >=1 more distinct structural token than the I2S blocking seeds. grimoire_tags >= 2 is an absolute floor guarding against both arms being near-zero (non-discriminating). size_lift >= 1.0 requires grimoire's structure to be at least as large (it never shrinks to clear a structural gate). All three over named analyze_pair metrics only.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: Could come out the other way: cmplog's I2S can substitute the 4-byte table-tag literals directly (that is exactly what input-to-state does), so if the gate were cleared by placing the tag LITERAL rather than by recombining a coherent multi-table structure, cmplog_tags would equal or exceed grimoire_tags and tag_lift -> 0, refuting the hypothesis. The test thus rules out the alternative 'grimoire wins only because it places the magic literal, a byte capability cmplog shares' and the alternative 'grimoire blocks-then-resolves for an unrelated scheduling/energy reason while both corpora carry equal structure'.
hypotheses.g2_independence: The shape's label SOURCE is the resolve pattern (grimoire resolves / cmplog blocks). The verification SIGNAL is the distinct-structural-token count + median size of the seed BYTES, never re-reading whether the branch flipped. Token presence is a byte property orthogonal to the resolve outcome, satisfying G2. (It is measured on cmplog's BLOCKING seeds vs grimoire's RESOLVING seeds, but the metric is structural content, not the block/resolve flag.)

hypotheses.label: fallback: grimoire reaches the gate via larger/deeper structure (state-machine depth, repeated category blocks, nested clusters) without a countable target-vocabulary tag signal

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: Same grimoire-vs-cmplog repoint; consumes only the size_lift metric (a subset of analyze_pair's output). For harfbuzz branches whose gate is a STATE/DEPTH property (Indic/Thai state-machine cases 5810/5866/5958, cluster-merge loop 5311) rather than a named table tag — these are recombined byte-run STRUCTURE, not a 4-byte sfnt tag the token list catches.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.3 (grimoire seeds materially larger/deeper) even when tag_lift is ~0, because the gate is about state-machine DEPTH / repeated category structure rather than a countable sfnt table tag (see harfbuzz_5810_h1 repeated 'f2 a8 00 00' dword block, harfbuzz_5866_h2 symbol-cluster recombination). A byte-havoc-only alternative predicts size_lift ~ 1.0 (no systematic size advantage); I2S-only (cmplog) also has no structural-growth bias, so a size advantage over the I2S arm discriminates structural recombination from local literal fixing.
hypotheses.rule_explanation: Consulted by the arbiter ONLY when structural_token_richness does NOT fire (its tag signal is flat) — see decision_order. Catches the state-machine-depth branches the table-tag vocab misses.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: If grimoire and cmplog seeds are the same size, size_lift -> ~1.0 and this is refuted.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Median seed size is a byte-length property of the corpus, independent of the resolve label.

harfbuzz.evidence: DB carries grimoire-RESOLVING seeds for all 6 (5311 n=9, 5440 n=10, 5810 n=2, 5866 n=2, 5958 n=8, 6181 n=10) AND cmplog-BLOCKING seeds for all 6 (n=10 each). analyze_pair requires W>=1 and L>=3 — satisfied for all 6 (5810/5866 grimoire n=2 still pass the W>=1 floor). on-disk harfbuzz corpus exists (data_reality 4: curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3). This round-1 design FIXES the round-0 measurability gap: round-0 needed naive-blocking seeds (5440 had none -> inconclusive; others had naive only as a RESOLVING arm), whereas cmplog is the true decisive blocking arm and is present for ALL 6.
harfbuzz.round0_delta: round-0 reported 5440 as inconclusive (no naive seeds) and weakened the contrast on 5311/5810/5866/5958 (naive co-resolved). Round-1 measures all 6 against the correct cmplog blocking arm.

libxml2.reason: Verified on this server: (1) 35/36 libxml2 grimoire branches have ZERO resolving_seeds AND ZERO blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite; the lone exception libxml2_6210 carries only value_profile-resolving / value_profile_cmplog-blocking seeds — NOT the grimoire-resolving nor cmplog-blocking arms this test needs. (2) libxml2 is NOT in the on-disk canonical-corpus set (data_reality 4: only curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3). (3) The only libxml2 corpus present is out/rq3/libxml2, which is the RQ3 fuzzer set (aflplusplus/honggfuzz/libafl/libfuzzer) with a GENERIC cmplog-only libafl build — it has NO grimoire arm at all, so no grimoire-vs-cmplog structural contrast can be drawn from it. (4) None of these 36 grimoire branch_ids have per_role_coverage W/L dumps (db/per_role_coverage/libxml2 exists but is keyed on a disjoint old branch_id namespace). No grimoire corpus + no DB seeds => no struct_size_and_token_count input.
libxml2.g3_server_note: This decidable:false is NOT merely an off-server-corpus artifact. sB owns the libxml2 corpus, but there is no GRIMOIRE-instrumented libxml2 campaign anywhere in this layout (the canonical 4-fuzzer campaign does not include libxml2; rq3's libafl is generic/cmplog-only). Arbitrating these would require BOTH a new grimoire-vs-cmplog libxml2 campaign AND a fresh seed_bisect to map per-branch W/L seeds — neither exists. So the 36 libxml2 branches are an HONEST data limitation (decidable:false), correctly carried to fallback:inconclusive, not forced into a label by analogy.

notes: ROUND-1 RE-DESIGN of grimoire_structural_WL after round-0 refutation. Round-0 ('structural_token_assembly' + 'structural_depth_only', repointed to grimoire-vs-NAIVE) was REFUTED: arbiter on the 31 inconclusive branches returned all rule_not_met (libxml2 28, harfbuzz 3). 11 branches WERE labeled by round-0, so a structural-assembly mechanism is REAL for part of the shape. THE TWO ROUND-1 FIXES: (1) WRONG BLOCKING ARM. Every card's covers_pairs is 'grimoire>cmplog', so the decisive blocking arm is CMPLOG, not naive. Round-0 contrasted grimoire-vs-naive, but the DB shows naive is in the RESOLVING set for 4/6 harfbuzz branches (naive co-resolved late) and entirely ABSENT for 5440 — so round-0's contrast was wrong-arm, self-weakening, and dropped 5440 to inconclusive. cmplog-BLOCKING seeds exist for ALL 6 measurable branches. (2) RICHNESS-NOT-SIZE FRAMING. The pivot is correct: grimoire's signature is <GAP>-token splicing / structural recombination, so the discriminating signal is TOKEN-RICHNESS (distinct structural constructs per seed), measured against the I2S arm. cmplog/I2S CAN substitute a single tag literal but CANNOT recombine multiple coherent constructs — so a positive tag_lift vs cmplog (rather than vs naive) isolates grammar-aware MULTI-TOKEN recombination from byte-level literal substitution. This is a STRONGER G1 discriminator than round-0's grimoire-vs-naive: naive trivially has no structure (an uninformative win), whereas cmplog is the hard, mechanism-relevant baseline that shares the literal-placement capability but lacks structural recombination. MERGES (unchanged from round-0, still correct): all 8 prior clusters collapse to ONE token-richness hypothesis plus one size-only fallback. The 8 labels (namespace_declaration_assembly / degenerate_field_collapse / multi_construct_co_occurrence / dtd_declaration_multi_construct / font_multi_table_assembly / element_tree_topology / token_preservation / quantitative_accumulation) all describe ONE technique-effect — grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural splice/recurse mutators place coherent grammar tokens/constructs cmplog's I2S cannot recombine; only the specific construct + gate differ. No real-data measurement separates 'assembles a namespace decl' from 'assembles an ATTLIST' from 'assembles a multi-table font'; all reduce to 'grimoire corpus is structurally richer than the I2S blocking corpus' (tag_lift>0). Keeping 8 clusters would be 8 copies of one rule. structural_size_depth_only stays SEPARATE only because state-machine-DEPTH branches (Indic/Thai cases, cluster-merge loop) encode the construct as depth/repeated-byte-runs rather than a countable sfnt tag — a real-data-separable size-vs-token-vocab distinction. THE DOMINANT REALITY (G3): this shape is 86% libxml2 (36/42) and libxml2 is UNMEASURABLE on the corpus/seed axis on BOTH servers (no grimoire-instrumented libxml2 campaign exists anywhere; rq3 libafl is generic cmplog-only) — verified, not assumed. Those 36 carry to fallback:inconclusive as an HONEST decidable:false data limitation, NOT a refutation and NOT an off-server-only artifact. The evidence test can confirm/refute the grimoire-structural mechanism only on the 6 harfbuzz branches; transfer to libxml2 would be by-analogy and is deliberately NOT asserted (G3: don't force labels). A future descriptor sourced from a grimoire-vs-cmplog libxml2 campaign (registry_tool:null, requires a new campaign + seed_bisect) could recover the 36 — out of scope for this round. CAVEAT: 5810/5866 have only n=2 grimoire resolving seeds — analyze_pair passes (W>=1) but the per-seed token mean is over a thin sample; the token rule (decision_order first) is more robust here than size_lift on n=2, which is noisier.
